docs.title: Aggregators
docs.text: Aggregate raw trades into bars using different policies. All functions accept numpy arrays and release the GIL.

docs.title: Common Parameters
docs.text:
All aggregation functions share the same input signature:

Parameter Type Description timestamps int64[] Trade timestamps in nanoseconds prices float64[] Trade prices quantities float64[] Trade quantities is_buy uint8[] Buy/sell flag (1 = buy, 0 = sell)

Returns: numpy.ndarray with PyExtBar structured dtype.

docs.title: PyExtBar Dtype
docs.text: Field Type Description start_time_ns int64 Bar open timestamp (unix ns) end_time_ns int64 Bar close timestamp (unix ns) open_raw int64 Open price * 10^8 high_raw int64 High price * 10^8 low_raw int64 Low price * 10^8 close_raw int64 Close price * 10^8 volume_raw int64 Total volume * 10^8 buy_volume_raw int64 Buy volume * 10^8 trade_count int64 Number of trades in bar

docs.title: aggregate_time_bars(..., interval_seconds)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into fixed time intervals.

bars = flox.aggregate_time_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                 interval_seconds=60.0)
Parameter Type Description interval_seconds float Bar duration in seconds

docs.title: aggregate_tick_bars(..., tick_count)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into bars with a fixed number of trades.

bars = flox.aggregate_tick_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                 tick_count=100)
Parameter Type Description tick_count int Trades per bar

docs.title: aggregate_volume_bars(..., volume_threshold)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into bars when cumulative volume exceeds a threshold.

bars = flox.aggregate_volume_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                   volume_threshold=1000.0)
Parameter Type Description volume_threshold float Volume per bar

docs.title: aggregate_range_bars(..., range_size)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into bars when price range exceeds a threshold.

bars = flox.aggregate_range_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                  range_size=10.0)
Parameter Type Description range_size float Maximum price range per bar

docs.title: aggregate_renko_bars(..., brick_size)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into Renko bars with a fixed brick size.

bars = flox.aggregate_renko_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                  brick_size=50.0)
Parameter Type Description brick_size float Renko brick size

docs.title: aggregate_heikin_ashi_bars(..., interval_seconds)
docs.text:
Aggregate trades into Heikin-Ashi bars (smoothed candlesticks).

bars = flox.aggregate_heikin_ashi_bars(timestamps, prices, quantities, is_buy,
                                        interval_seconds=60.0)
Parameter Type Description interval_seconds float Bar duration in seconds

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
import numpy as np
import flox_py as flox

# Load trade data
reader = flox.DataReader("./data")
trades = reader.read_trades()

ts = trades['exchange_ts_ns']
px = trades['price_raw'] / 1e8
qty = trades['qty_raw'] / 1e8
side = trades['side']

# Create 1-minute time bars
bars = flox.aggregate_time_bars(ts, px, qty, side, interval_seconds=60.0)
print(f"Generated {len(bars)} time bars")

# Create 500-tick bars
tick_bars = flox.aggregate_tick_bars(ts, px, qty, side, tick_count=500)
print(f"Generated {len(tick_bars)} tick bars")

# Access bar data
opens = bars['open_raw'] / 1e8
highs = bars['high_raw'] / 1e8
lows = bars['low_raw'] / 1e8
closes = bars['close_raw'] / 1e8

docs.title: Backtest Components
docs.text: Standalone simulated exchange for step-by-step backtesting and fill analysis.

docs.title: SimulatedExecutor
docs.text:
A simulated exchange that matches orders against bar closes and trade prices. Supports market, limit, stop, take-profit, and trailing stop orders.

executor = flox.SimulatedExecutor()

docs.title: submit_order(id, side, price, quantity, type="market", symbol=1)
docs.text:
Submit an order to the simulated exchange.

executor.submit_order(id=1, side="buy", price=0.0, quantity=1.0,
                       type="market", symbol=1)
Parameter Type Default Description id int — Order ID side str — "buy" or "sell" price float — Limit/trigger price (0 for market) quantity float — Order size type str "market" Order type (see below) symbol int 1 Symbol ID

Order types: "market", "limit", "stop_market", "stop_limit", "take_profit_market", "take_profit_limit", "trailing_stop".

docs.title: cancel_order(order_id)
docs.text: Cancel an order by ID.

docs.title: cancel_all(symbol)
docs.text: Cancel all orders for a symbol.

docs.title: on_bar(symbol, close_price)
docs.text:
Feed a bar close price for order matching. Limit orders are matched against this price.

executor.on_bar(symbol=1, close_price=50000.0)

docs.title: on_trade(symbol, price, is_buy)
docs.text:
Feed a trade for order matching.

executor.on_trade(symbol=1, price=50000.0, is_buy=True)

docs.title: on_trade_qty(symbol, price, qty, is_buy)
docs.text:
Feed a trade with quantity. Required for QUEUE_FULL queue simulation.

executor.on_trade_qty(symbol=1, price=50000.0, qty=0.5, is_buy=True)

docs.title: on_best_levels(symbol, bid_price, bid_qty, ask_price, ask_qty)
docs.text:
Feed a top-of-book snapshot.

executor.on_best_levels(1, 49999.0, 2.0, 50001.0, 1.5)

docs.title: set_default_slippage(model, ticks=0, tick_size=0.0, bps=0.0, impact_coeff=0.0)
docs.text:
Configure slippage for all symbols.

from flox import SLIPPAGE_FIXED_BPS
executor.set_default_slippage(SLIPPAGE_FIXED_BPS, bps=2.0)
model constant Value Description SLIPPAGE_NONE 0 No slippage SLIPPAGE_FIXED_TICKS 1 Fixed tick count SLIPPAGE_FIXED_BPS 2 Fixed basis points SLIPPAGE_VOLUME_IMPACT 3 Volume-proportional impact

docs.title: set_symbol_slippage(symbol, model, ticks=0, tick_size=0.0, bps=0.0, impact_coeff=0.0)
docs.text: Per-symbol slippage override. Same parameters as set_default_slippage.

docs.title: set_queue_model(model, depth=1)
docs.text:
Configure limit order queue simulation.

from flox import QUEUE_TOB
executor.set_queue_model(QUEUE_TOB, depth=1)
model constant Value Description QUEUE_NONE 0 Fill limit orders immediately at price QUEUE_TOB 1 Fill only when price trades through level QUEUE_FULL 2 Model queue position; fill as volume passes

docs.title: advance_clock(timestamp_ns)
docs.text:
Advance the simulation clock.

executor.advance_clock(timestamp_ns=1704067200_000_000_000)

docs.title: fills() -> ndarray
docs.text:
Get all fills as a numpy structured array with PyFill dtype.

fills = executor.fills()
for i in range(len(fills)):
    print(f"Fill: order={fills[i]['order_id']}, price={fills[i]['price_raw']/1e8}")

docs.title: fills_list() -> list[dict]
docs.text:
Get fills as a list of dicts (more convenient, less performant).

for fill in executor.fills_list():
    print(f"{fill['side']} {fill['quantity']} @ {fill['price']}")
Key Type Description order_id int Order ID symbol int Symbol ID side str "buy" or "sell" price float Fill price quantity float Fill quantity timestamp_ns int Fill timestamp

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description fill_count int Number of fills

docs.title: BacktestResult
docs.text:
Computes statistics and equity curve from a SimulatedExecutor's fills.

result = flox.BacktestResult(initial_capital=10_000.0, fee_rate=0.0004)
result.ingest_executor(executor)
stats = result.stats()
print(stats['net_pnl'], stats['sharpe'])

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
flox.BacktestResult(
    initial_capital=100000.0,
    fee_rate=0.0001,
    use_percentage_fee=True,
    fixed_fee_per_trade=0.0,
    risk_free_rate=0.0,
    annualization_factor=252.0,
)

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
Method Returns Description record_fill(order_id, symbol, side, price, qty, timestamp_ns) None Record a single fill ingest_executor(executor) None Drain all fills from a SimulatedExecutor stats() dict Compute and return statistics equity_curve_size() int Number of equity curve points write_equity_curve_csv(path) bool Write equity curve to CSV close() None Free resources

stats() returns the same keys as Engine.run() plus start_time_ns and end_time_ns.

docs.title: PyFill
docs.text: Field Type Description order_id uint64 Order ID symbol uint32 Symbol ID side uint8 0 = buy, 1 = sell price_raw int64 Fill price * 10^8 quantity_raw int64 Fill quantity * 10^8 timestamp_ns int64 Fill timestamp (ns)

docs.title: PyTradeRecord
docs.text: Field Type Description symbol uint32 Symbol ID side uint8 0 = buy, 1 = sell entry_price_raw int64 Entry price * 10^8 exit_price_raw int64 Exit price * 10^8 quantity_raw int64 Trade quantity * 10^8 entry_time_ns int64 Entry timestamp exit_time_ns int64 Exit timestamp pnl_raw int64 PnL * 10^8 fee_raw int64 Fee * 10^8

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
import flox_py as flox

executor = flox.SimulatedExecutor()

# Simulate a simple trade
executor.advance_clock(1_000_000_000)  # t=1s
executor.submit_order(id=1, side="buy", price=0, quantity=1.0)
executor.on_bar(symbol=1, close_price=50000.0)

executor.advance_clock(2_000_000_000)  # t=2s
executor.submit_order(id=2, side="sell", price=0, quantity=1.0)
executor.on_bar(symbol=1, close_price=51000.0)

for fill in executor.fills_list():
    print(f"{fill['side']} {fill['quantity']} @ {fill['price']}")

docs.title: Order Books
docs.text: Order book implementations for managing price levels and simulating market impact.

docs.title: OrderBook
docs.text:
N-level order book with snapshot and delta support. Backed by the C++ NLevelOrderBook<8192>.

book = flox.OrderBook(tick_size=0.01)
Parameter Type Description tick_size float Minimum price increment

docs.title: apply_snapshot(bid_prices, bid_quantities, ask_prices, ask_quantities)
docs.text:
Apply a full book snapshot (replaces existing book state).

book.apply_snapshot(
    bid_prices=np.array([100.0, 99.99, 99.98]),
    bid_quantities=np.array([10.0, 25.0, 15.0]),
    ask_prices=np.array([100.01, 100.02, 100.03]),
    ask_quantities=np.array([8.0, 20.0, 12.0]),
)

docs.title: apply_delta(bid_prices, bid_quantities, ask_prices, ask_quantities)
docs.text:
Apply an incremental update (quantity=0 removes level).

book.apply_delta(
    bid_prices=np.array([100.0]),
    bid_quantities=np.array([15.0]),  # update qty
    ask_prices=np.array([100.01]),
    ask_quantities=np.array([0.0]),   # remove level
)

bids = book.get_bids(max_levels=10)
# bids[0] = [best_bid_price, best_bid_qty]

Simulate a market buy order. Returns (filled_qty, total_cost).

filled, cost = book.consume_asks(100.0)
avg_price = cost / filled if filled > 0 else 0

docs.text: Simulate a market sell order. Returns (filled_qty, total_cost).

Level-3 order book with individual order tracking. Backed by L3OrderBook<8192>.

docs.text:
Add an order. Returns status: "ok", "no_capacity", "extant".

status = book.add_order(order_id=1, price=100.0, quantity=10.0, side="buy")

Export all orders as a list of dicts.

orders = book.export_snapshot()
# [{"id": 1, "price": 100.0, "quantity": 10.0, "side": "buy"}, ...]

docs.text:
Rebuild the book from a snapshot.

book.build_from_snapshot([
    {"id": 1, "price": 100.0, "quantity": 10.0, "side": "buy"},
    {"id": 2, "price": 100.01, "quantity": 5.0, "side": "sell"},

docs.title: CompositeBookMatrix
docs.text:
Cross-exchange composite order book. Tracks best bid/ask across up to 4 exchanges per symbol with lock-free atomic reads.

matrix = flox.CompositeBookMatrix(staleness_threshold_ms=5000)
Parameter Type Default Description staleness_threshold_ms int 5000 Time in ms before a quote is considered stale

docs.title: update_book(exchange, symbol, bid_prices, bid_quantities, ask_prices, ask_quantities, recv_ns=0)
docs.text:
Feed a book update from an exchange.

matrix.update_book(
    exchange=0, symbol=1,
    bid_prices=np.array([50000.0]),
    bid_quantities=np.array([1.5]),
    ask_prices=np.array([50001.0]),
    ask_quantities=np.array([2.0]),
    recv_ns=1704067200_000_000_000,
)

docs.title: best_bid(symbol) -> dict | None
docs.text:
Best bid across all non-stale exchanges. Returns {"price": float, "quantity": float, "exchange": int} or None.

bid = matrix.best_bid(symbol=1)
if bid:
    print(f"Best bid: {bid['price']} on exchange {bid['exchange']}")

docs.title: best_ask(symbol) -> dict | None
docs.text: Best ask across all non-stale exchanges.

docs.title: bid_for_exchange(symbol, exchange) -> dict | None
docs.text: Best bid on a specific exchange.

docs.title: ask_for_exchange(symbol, exchange) -> dict | None
docs.text: Best ask on a specific exchange.

docs.title: has_arbitrage_opportunity(symbol) -> bool
docs.text:
Returns True if the best bid on one exchange exceeds the best ask on another.

if matrix.has_arbitrage_opportunity(symbol=1):
    bid = matrix.best_bid(1)
    ask = matrix.best_ask(1)
    profit = bid['price'] - ask['price']
    print(f"Arb: buy on ex{ask['exchange']} sell on ex{bid['exchange']}, profit={profit}")

docs.title: spread(symbol) -> float | None
docs.text: Composite spread (best ask - best bid), or None if no valid quotes.

docs.title: mark_stale(exchange, symbol)
docs.text: Mark a specific exchange+symbol pair as stale (excluded from best quote queries).

docs.title: mark_exchange_stale(exchange)
docs.text: Mark all symbols on an exchange as stale (e.g., on disconnect).

docs.title: check_staleness(now_ns)
docs.text:
Check all entries against the staleness threshold configured at construction.

import time
matrix.check_staleness(int(time.time_ns()))

docs.title: Engine & Backtest
docs.text: The core backtest engine. Load OHLCV data once, then run unlimited backtests with different signal sets.

docs.title: Engine
docs.text:
engine = flox.Engine(initial_capital=100_000, fee_rate=0.0001)
Parameter Type Default Description initial_capital float 100000.0 Starting capital fee_rate float 0.0001 Fee rate per trade (percentage mode)

docs.title: load_bars(bars)
docs.text:
Load bars from a numpy structured array with PyBar dtype.

bars = np.zeros(n, dtype=flox.PyBar)
bars['timestamp_ns'] = timestamps
bars['open_raw'] = (opens * 1e8).astype(np.int64)
bars['high_raw'] = (highs * 1e8).astype(np.int64)
bars['low_raw'] = (lows * 1e8).astype(np.int64)
bars['close_raw'] = (closes * 1e8).astype(np.int64)
bars['volume_raw'] = (volumes * 1e8).astype(np.int64)
engine.load_bars(bars)

docs.title: load_bars_df(timestamps, open, high, low, close, volume)
docs.text:
Load bars from separate numpy arrays (float64). Timestamps are auto-normalized to nanoseconds.

engine.load_bars_df(timestamps, opens, highs, lows, closes, volumes)
Parameter Type Description timestamps int64[] Unix timestamps (s, ms, us, or ns — auto-detected) open float64[] Open prices high float64[] High prices low float64[] Low prices close float64[] Close prices volume float64[] Volume

docs.title: run(signals, symbol=1) -> dict
docs.text:
Run a single backtest. Returns a stats dictionary.

stats = engine.run(signals, symbol=1)
print(stats['net_pnl'], stats['sharpe'])

Return keys:

Key Type Description total_trades int Round-trip trade count winning_trades int Profitable trade count losing_trades int Losing trade count initial_capital float Starting capital final_capital float Ending capital total_pnl float Gross PnL total_fees float Total fees paid net_pnl float PnL after fees gross_profit float Sum of winning trades gross_loss float Sum of losing trades max_drawdown float Maximum drawdown (absolute) max_drawdown_pct float Maximum drawdown (percentage) win_rate float Fraction of winning trades profit_factor float Gross profit / gross loss avg_win float Average winning trade avg_loss float Average losing trade sharpe float Annualized Sharpe ratio sortino float Annualized Sortino ratio calmar float Calmar ratio return_pct float Net return percentage

docs.title: run_batch(signal_sets, threads=0, symbol=1) -> list[dict]
docs.text:
Run N backtests in parallel using C++ threads. GIL released.

results = engine.run_batch([signals_1, signals_2, ...], threads=0)
Parameter Type Default Description signal_sets list — List of signal arrays threads int 0 Thread count (0 = all cores) symbol int 1 Symbol ID

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description bar_count int Number of loaded bars

docs.title: make_signals()
docs.text:
Create a packed signal array from separate numpy arrays.

signals = flox.make_signals(
    timestamps,   # int64 — unix ms, us, or ns
    sides,        # uint8 — 0=buy, 1=sell
    quantities,   # float64 — position size
    prices=None,  # float64 — limit price (optional)
    types=None,   # uint8 — 0=market, 1=limit (optional)
)
Parameter Type Default Description timestamps int64[] — Trade timestamps (auto-normalized to ns) sides uint8[] — 0 = buy, 1 = sell quantities float64[] — Position sizes prices float64[] None Limit prices (omit for market orders) types uint8[] None Order types: 0 = market, 1 = limit

Returns: numpy.ndarray with PySignal dtype.

docs.title: PySignal
docs.text: Field Type Description timestamp_ns int64 Timestamp in nanoseconds quantity_raw int64 Quantity * 10^8 price_raw int64 Price * 10^8 (0 for market) side uint8 0 = buy, 1 = sell order_type uint8 0 = market, 1 = limit

docs.title: PyBar
docs.text: Field Type Description timestamp_ns int64 Bar timestamp in nanoseconds open_raw int64 Open * 10^8 high_raw int64 High * 10^8 low_raw int64 Low * 10^8 close_raw int64 Close * 10^8 volume_raw int64 Volume * 10^8

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
import numpy as np
import flox_py as flox

engine = flox.Engine(initial_capital=100_000, fee_rate=0.0001)
engine.load_bars_df(timestamps, opens, highs, lows, closes, volumes)

# Simple MA crossover signals
fast = flox.ema(closes, 10)
slow = flox.ema(closes, 30)

cross_up = (fast[1:] > slow[1:]) & (fast[:-1] <= slow[:-1])
cross_down = (fast[1:] < slow[1:]) & (fast[:-1] >= slow[:-1])

ts_list, side_list, qty_list = [], [], []
for i in range(len(cross_up)):
    if cross_up[i]:
        ts_list.append(timestamps[i + 1])
        side_list.append(0)  # buy
        qty_list.append(1.0)
    elif cross_down[i]:
        ts_list.append(timestamps[i + 1])
        side_list.append(1)  # sell
        qty_list.append(1.0)

signals = flox.make_signals(
    np.array(ts_list, dtype=np.int64),
    np.array(side_list, dtype=np.uint8),
    np.array(qty_list, dtype=np.float64),
)

stats = engine.run(signals)
print(f"Net PnL: {stats['net_pnl']:.2f}, Sharpe: {stats['sharpe']:.4f}")

docs.title: Indicators
docs.text: Vectorized technical indicators operating on numpy arrays. All functions release the GIL for parallel computation.

docs.title: Streaming classes
docs.text:
All streaming indicators share the same interface:

ind = flox.EMA(20)
result = ind.update(price)  # None during warmup, float when ready
ind.value                   # None or float
ind.ready                   # bool
ind.reset()                 # clear state, keep config
ind2 = ind.fresh()          # new independent instance, same config

During warmup, update() and .value return None. Check with if result is not None or use .ready.

Single value — update(value) -> float | None:

SMA(period), EMA(period), RMA(period), RSI(period), DEMA(period), TEMA(period), KAMA(period, fast=2, slow=30), Slope(length), Skewness(period), Kurtosis(period), RollingZScore(period), ShannonEntropy(period, bins=10)

Multi-output — update(value) -> float | None, named properties instead of .value:

MACD(fast=12, slow=26, signal=9) → .line, .signal, .histogram Bollinger(period=20, multiplier=2.0) → .upper, .middle, .lower

OHLC / multi-input:

ATR(period) — update(high, low, close) Stochastic(k_period=14, d_period=3) — update(high, low, close) → .k, .d CCI(period=20) — update(high, low, close) ParkinsonVol(period) — update(high, low) RogersSatchellVol(period) — update(open, high, low, close) Correlation(period) — update(x, y)

Volume:

OBV() — update(close, volume) VWAP(window) — update(close, volume) CVD() — update(open, high, low, close, volume)

docs.title: ema(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Exponential Moving Average.

result = flox.ema(closes, period=20)

docs.title: sma(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Simple Moving Average.

result = flox.sma(closes, period=20)

docs.title: rma(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Wilder's Moving Average (alpha = 1/period). Used internally by RSI and ATR.

result = flox.rma(closes, period=14)

docs.title: dema(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Double EMA: 2*EMA - EMA(EMA). Reduces lag versus standard EMA.

result = flox.dema(closes, period=20)

docs.title: tema(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Triple EMA: 3*EMA - 3*EMA(EMA) + EMA(EMA(EMA)). Further lag reduction.

result = flox.tema(closes, period=20)

docs.title: kama(input, period=10) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Kaufman Adaptive Moving Average. Adjusts smoothing based on market efficiency.

result = flox.kama(closes, period=10)

docs.title: rsi(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Relative Strength Index (0–100).

result = flox.rsi(closes, period=14)

docs.title: macd(input, fast=12, slow=26, signal=9) -> dict
docs.text:
Moving Average Convergence Divergence. Returns a dict with three arrays.

result = flox.macd(closes, fast=12, slow=26, signal=9)
# result['line'], result['signal'], result['histogram']

docs.title: stochastic(high, low, close, k_period=14, d_period=3) -> dict
docs.text:
Stochastic oscillator (%K and %D).

result = flox.stochastic(highs, lows, closes, k_period=14, d_period=3)
k = result['k']
d = result['d']

docs.title: cci(high, low, close, period=20) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Commodity Channel Index.

result = flox.cci(highs, lows, closes, period=20)

docs.title: adx(high, low, close, period=14) -> dict
docs.text:
Average Directional Index with directional indicators.

result = flox.adx(highs, lows, closes, period=14)
# result['adx'], result['plus_di'], result['minus_di']

docs.title: chop(high, low, close, period=14) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Choppiness Index (0–100). High values indicate ranging markets.

result = flox.chop(highs, lows, closes, period=14)

docs.title: slope(input, length=1) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Linear slope over a lookback window.

result = flox.slope(closes, length=5)

docs.title: atr(high, low, close, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Average True Range.

result = flox.atr(highs, lows, closes, period=14)

docs.title: bollinger(input, period=20, stddev=2.0) -> dict
docs.text:
Bollinger Bands.

result = flox.bollinger(closes, period=20, stddev=2.0)
# result['upper'], result['middle'], result['lower']

docs.title: skewness(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling Fisher-Pearson skewness. Measures distribution asymmetry. Requires period >= 3. NaN if std = 0.

result = flox.skewness(closes, period=20)

docs.title: kurtosis(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling Fisher excess kurtosis. Measures tail heaviness. Requires period >= 4. NaN if std = 0.

result = flox.kurtosis(closes, period=20)

docs.title: rolling_zscore(input, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling z-score normalization: (x - mean) / std. NaN if std = 0.

result = flox.rolling_zscore(closes, period=20)

docs.title: shannon_entropy(input, period, bins=10) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling Shannon entropy with histogram binning, normalized to [0, 1]. Zero = all values identical, 1 = uniform distribution.

result = flox.shannon_entropy(closes, period=20, bins=10)

docs.title: parkinson_vol(high, low, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Parkinson high-low volatility estimator: sqrt(mean(ln(H/L)^2) / (4*ln(2))). More efficient than close-to-close volatility.

result = flox.parkinson_vol(highs, lows, period=20)

docs.title: rogers_satchell_vol(open, high, low, close, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rogers-Satchell OHLC volatility estimator. Unbiased with drift, suitable for trending markets.

result = flox.rogers_satchell_vol(opens, highs, lows, closes, period=20)

docs.title: rolling_correlation(x, y, period) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling Pearson correlation between two series. NaN if either series is constant within the window.

flox.correlation(x, y) (no period) is the single-number Pearson coefficient — see Optimizer.

result = flox.rolling_correlation(closes_btc, closes_eth, period=20)

docs.title: vwap(close, volume, window=96) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Rolling Volume-Weighted Average Price.

result = flox.vwap(closes, volumes, window=96)

docs.title: obv(close, volume) -> ndarray
docs.text:
On-Balance Volume.

result = flox.obv(closes, volumes)

docs.title: cvd(open, high, low, close, volume) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Cumulative Volume Delta. Estimates buying/selling pressure from OHLCV data.

result = flox.cvd(opens, highs, lows, closes, volumes)

docs.title: bar_returns(signal_long, signal_short, log_returns) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Compute per-bar returns given position signals and log returns.

returns = flox.bar_returns(signal_long, signal_short, log_returns)

signal_long and signal_short are int8[] (+1 or 0 / -1 or 0). log_returns is float64[].

docs.title: trade_pnl(signal_long, signal_short, log_returns) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Extract per-trade PnL from position signals.

pnls = flox.trade_pnl(signal_long, signal_short, log_returns)

docs.title: profit_factor(returns) -> float
docs.text:
Ratio of gross profit to gross loss. Values > 1.0 indicate profitability.

pf = flox.profit_factor(returns)

docs.title: win_rate(trade_pnls) -> float
docs.text:
Fraction of trades with positive PnL.

wr = flox.win_rate(pnls)

docs.title: Indicator catalog
docs.text:
Every indicator below is one Python class with both a batch compute() method and streaming update()/value/ready/reset(). Same instance, two ways to use it:

import flox_py as flox
ema = flox.EMA(10)
out = ema.compute(prices)             # batch
for v in stream:
    ema.update(v)
    if ema.ready: print(ema.value)    # streaming on the same instance
Indicator Constructor Kind EMA flox.EMA(period) SingleInput SMA flox.SMA(period) SingleInput RMA flox.RMA(period) SingleInput RSI flox.RSI(period) SingleInput KAMA flox.KAMA(period, fast, slow) SingleInput DEMA flox.DEMA(period) SingleInput TEMA flox.TEMA(period) SingleInput Slope flox.Slope(length) SingleInput Skewness flox.Skewness(period) SingleInput Kurtosis flox.Kurtosis(period) SingleInput RollingZScore flox.RollingZScore(period) SingleInput ShannonEntropy flox.ShannonEntropy(period, bins) SingleInput AutoCorrelation flox.AutoCorrelation(window, lag) SingleInput ATR flox.ATR(period) BarInput CCI flox.CCI(period) BarInput Stochastic flox.Stochastic(k_period, d_period) BarInput ParkinsonVol flox.ParkinsonVol(period) HighLowInput RogersSatchellVol flox.RogersSatchellVol(period) OhlcInput Correlation flox.Correlation(period) PairInput MACD flox.MACD(fast, slow, signal) MultiOutput Bollinger flox.Bollinger(period, stddev) MultiOutput

Discovery: flox.list_indicators() returns the full list at runtime.

docs.title: Optimizer
docs.text: Statistical tools for strategy validation and parameter optimization.

docs.title: permutation_test()
docs.text:
Two-sample permutation test. Tests whether two groups have the same mean. Useful for comparing strategy returns against random shuffles.

p_value = flox.permutation_test(group1, group2, num_permutations=10000)
Parameter Type Default Description group1 float64[] — First sample group2 float64[] — Second sample num_permutations int 10000 Number of permutation iterations

Returns: float — p-value (probability of observing the actual difference by chance).

# Test if strategy returns are significantly different from random
strategy_returns = np.array([0.01, 0.02, -0.005, 0.015, ...])
random_returns = np.array([0.001, -0.003, 0.002, -0.001, ...])

p = flox.permutation_test(strategy_returns, random_returns)
print(f"p-value: {p:.4f}")
if p < 0.05:
    print("Strategy returns are statistically significant")

docs.title: correlation()
docs.text:
Pearson correlation coefficient between two arrays.

r = flox.correlation(x, y)
Parameter Type Description x float64[] First variable y float64[] Second variable (same length as x)

Returns: float — correlation coefficient in [-1, 1].

# Check parameter sensitivity
param_values = np.array([10, 20, 30, 40, 50], dtype=np.float64)
sharpe_ratios = np.array([0.5, 1.2, 1.8, 1.5, 0.8])

r = flox.correlation(param_values, sharpe_ratios)
print(f"Correlation: {r:.4f}")

docs.title: bootstrap_ci()
docs.text:
Bootstrap confidence interval for the mean. Resamples the data with replacement to estimate uncertainty.

lower, median, upper = flox.bootstrap_ci(data, confidence=0.95, num_samples=10000)
Parameter Type Default Description data float64[] — Sample data (must be non-empty) confidence float 0.95 Confidence level (e.g., 0.95 for 95%) num_samples int 10000 Bootstrap iterations

Returns: tuple[float, float, float] — (lower, median, upper) bounds.

# Estimate confidence interval for strategy Sharpe ratio
trade_pnls = np.array([100, -50, 200, -30, 150, ...], dtype=np.float64)

lower, median, upper = flox.bootstrap_ci(trade_pnls, confidence=0.95)
print(f"Mean PnL: {median:.2f} [{lower:.2f}, {upper:.2f}] (95% CI)")

docs.title: Example: Full Validation Pipeline
docs.text:
import numpy as np
import flox_py as flox

engine = flox.Engine(initial_capital=100_000)
engine.load_bars_df(timestamps, opens, highs, lows, closes, volumes)

# Run strategy
signals = my_strategy(closes)
base_stats = engine.run(signals)
base_pnl = base_stats['net_pnl']

# Monte Carlo permutation test
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
shuffled_sets = []
for _ in range(1000):
    idx = rng.permutation(len(signals))
    shuffled = signals[idx]
    shuffled_sets.append(shuffled)

results = engine.run_batch(shuffled_sets)
random_pnls = np.array([r['net_pnl'] for r in results])

# Statistical significance
p_value = np.mean(random_pnls >= base_pnl)
print(f"Strategy PnL: {base_pnl:.2f}, p-value: {p_value:.4f}")

# Confidence interval on trade PnLs
trade_pnls = flox.trade_pnl(signal_long, signal_short, log_returns)
lo, med, hi = flox.bootstrap_ci(trade_pnls)
print(f"Trade PnL: {med:.4f} [{lo:.4f}, {hi:.4f}]")

# Parameter sensitivity
param_sharpes = []
for period in range(5, 50):
    sigs = ma_strategy(closes, period)
    stats = engine.run(sigs)
    param_sharpes.append(stats['sharpe'])

r = flox.correlation(
    np.arange(5, 50, dtype=np.float64),
    np.array(param_sharpes),
)
print(f"Period-Sharpe correlation: {r:.4f}")

docs.title: MarketProfile
docs.text:
Market Profile (TPO) aggregator. Tracks price activity across time periods for auction theory analysis.

mp = flox.MarketProfile(tick_size=0.01, period_minutes=30, session_start_ns=start_ns)

docs.title: Replay

docs.title: inspect()
docs.text:
Quick summary of a data directory without loading all data.

info = flox.inspect(data_dir="./data")
print(f"Events: {info['total_events']}, Duration: {info['duration_seconds']:.0f}s")

Returns dict:

docs.title: DataReader
docs.text:
Read trades and book updates from binary log files.

reader = flox.DataReader(
    data_dir="./data",
    from_ns=None,             # start timestamp filter
    to_ns=None,               # end timestamp filter
    symbols=None,             # list of symbol IDs to filter
    reorder_window_ns=None,   # cross-block reorder window (default 10s)
)

reorder_window_ns controls the bounded reorder buffer used by run() and streamForEach on segments without the Sorted flag. Events arriving more than reorder_window_ns past the watermark raise FloxError(code="E_DATA_002"). See Aggregate tape events for details.

docs.title: summary() -> dict
docs.text: Dataset summary (same keys as inspect() plus data_dir and symbols).

docs.title: count() -> int
docs.text: Total event count.

docs.title: symbols() -> set[int]
docs.text: Set of available symbol IDs.

docs.title: time_range() -> tuple[int, int] | None
docs.text: (start_ns, end_ns) tuple, or None if empty.

docs.title: read_trades() -> ndarray
docs.text:
Read all trades as a numpy structured array with PyTrade dtype.

trades = reader.read_trades()
prices = trades['price_raw'] / 1e8
quantities = trades['qty_raw'] / 1e8

docs.title: read_trades_from(start_ts_ns) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Read trades starting from a given timestamp.

trades = reader.read_trades_from(start_ts_ns=1704067200_000_000_000)

docs.title: read_bbo() -> ndarray
docs.text:
Read top-of-book (best bid/ask) from every book update event as a numpy structured array with PyBBO dtype.

bbos = reader.read_bbo()
mids = (bbos['bid_price_raw'] + bbos['ask_price_raw']) / 2 / 1e8

docs.title: read_bbo_from(start_ts_ns) -> ndarray
docs.text:
Same as read_bbo() but starts iterating from the first event whose exchange_ts_ns >= start_ts_ns.

bbos = reader.read_bbo_from(start_ts_ns=1704067200_000_000_000)

docs.title: read_book_updates() -> tuple[ndarray, ndarray]
docs.text:
Read every book update event with full depth. Returns (headers, levels):

  • headers — structured array of PyBookUpdateHeader. Each row carries level_offset, bid_count, ask_count for slicing the levels array.
  • levels — single flat structured array of PyLevel shared by all events; bids first, then asks, per event.
headers, levels = reader.read_book_updates()
for h in headers:
    off, nb, na = h['level_offset'], h['bid_count'], h['ask_count']
    bids = levels[off : off + nb]
    asks = levels[off + nb : off + nb + na]

docs.title: read_book_updates_from(start_ts_ns) -> tuple[ndarray, ndarray]
docs.text:
Same as read_book_updates() but starts iterating from the first event whose exchange_ts_ns >= start_ts_ns.

headers, levels = reader.read_book_updates_from(start_ts_ns=1704067200_000_000_000)

docs.title: stats() -> dict
docs.text:
Reader statistics.

Key Type Description files_read int Segment files read events_read int Total events processed trades_read int Trade events book_updates_read int Book update events bytes_read int Bytes read crc_errors int CRC checksum failures

docs.title: run(aggregators, n_threads=0) -> bool
docs.text:
Single-pass streaming aggregator dispatch. Walks the tape once and forwards each event to every aggregator's onEvent, then calls finalize() once on each. The GIL is released for the whole walk; aggregators must be self-contained.

reader.run([stats, peaks, quantiles])           # n_threads=0 → auto
reader.run([stats, peaks, quantiles], 4)        # 4 workers
reader.run([stats, peaks, quantiles], 1)        # explicit single-thread

n_threads policy:

  • 0 (default): auto, resolved to min(blocks_per_segment / 2, hardware_concurrency()).
  • 1: explicit single-thread.
  • >1: explicit worker count, capped to the effective block count per segment.

Parallel mode partitions the segment at the compressed-block level. Each worker holds a panel cloned via cloneEmpty() and walks its assigned block range; results merge via merge() before finalize(). See Aggregate tape events for boundary semantics on sliding-window aggregators.

docs.title: segment_files() -> list[str]
docs.text: List of segment file paths.

docs.title: segments() -> list[dict]
docs.text:
Segment metadata.

Key Type Description path str File path first_event_ns int First event timestamp last_event_ns int Last event timestamp event_count int Events in segment has_index bool Whether segment has an index

docs.title: PyTrade Dtype
docs.text: Field Type Description exchange_ts_ns int64 Exchange timestamp (ns) recv_ts_ns int64 Local receive timestamp (ns) price_raw int64 Price * 10^8 qty_raw int64 Quantity * 10^8 trade_id uint64 Exchange trade ID symbol_id uint32 Symbol ID side uint8 0 = buy, 1 = sell

docs.title: PyBBO Dtype
docs.text: Field Type Description exchange_ts_ns int64 Exchange timestamp (ns) recv_ts_ns int64 Local receive timestamp (ns) seq int64 Exchange sequence number symbol_id uint32 Symbol ID event_type uint8 2 = snapshot, 3 = delta bid_price_raw int64 Best bid price * 10^8 (0 if absent) bid_qty_raw int64 Best bid quantity * 10^8 ask_price_raw int64 Best ask price * 10^8 (0 if absent) ask_qty_raw int64 Best ask quantity * 10^8

docs.title: PyBookUpdateHeader Dtype
docs.text: Field Type Description exchange_ts_ns int64 Exchange timestamp (ns) recv_ts_ns int64 Local receive timestamp (ns) seq int64 Exchange sequence number symbol_id uint32 Symbol ID bid_count uint16 Number of bid levels for this event ask_count uint16 Number of ask levels for this event level_offset uint32 Index of this event's first level in the levels array event_type uint8 2 = snapshot, 3 = delta

docs.title: PyLevel Dtype
docs.text: Field Type Description price_raw int64 Price * 10^8 qty_raw int64 Quantity * 10^8 side uint8 0 = bid, 1 = ask

docs.title: Strategy API
docs.text: Event-driven strategy classes for Python. Mirrors C++ flox::Strategy.

docs.title: Class: flox_py.Symbol
docs.text:
Returned by SymbolRegistry.add_symbol. Works transparently as an int wherever a symbol ID is expected.

Property Type Description id int Numeric symbol ID name str Symbol name, e.g. "BTCUSDT" exchange str Exchange name, e.g. "binance" tick_size float Minimum price increment 
int(btc)   # 1
print(btc) # Symbol(binance:BTCUSDT, id=1)

docs.title: Class: flox_py.SymbolRegistry
docs.text:
registry = flox.SymbolRegistry()
btc = registry.add_symbol("binance", "BTCUSDT", tick_size=0.01)
Method Returns Description add_symbol(exchange, name, tick_size) Symbol Register a symbol and return its Symbol object

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
Strategy(symbols: list[Symbol | int])

symbols — list of Symbol objects or raw integer IDs to subscribe to.

docs.title: on_trade(ctx: SymbolContext, trade: TradeData)
docs.text:
Called on each trade event for subscribed symbols.

def on_trade(self, ctx, trade):
    if ctx.is_flat():
        self.market_buy(0.01)

docs.title: on_book_update(ctx: SymbolContext)
docs.text: Called on each order book update.

docs.title: on_bar(ctx: SymbolContext, bar: BarData)
docs.text:
Called on each closed OHLC bar. bar exposes open, high, low, close, volume, buy_volume, start_time_ns, end_time_ns, bar_type, bar_type_param, close_reason. Use BacktestRunner.run_bars(...) to replay historical bars or Runner.on_bar(...) to push live bars.

def on_bar(self, ctx, bar):
    # detect breakout on bar close
    if bar.close > self.prev_high and ctx.is_flat():
        self.market_buy(0.01)
    self.prev_high = max(getattr(self, "prev_high", 0.0), bar.high)
